edges.description: Vitreous cells and haze obscure the visual axis causing floaters and blurred vision.
Peripheral vasculitis and leakage contribute to macular thickening that impairs vision.
Macular thickening degrades visual acuity in intermediate uveitis.
edges.source: Macular Thickening from Blood-Retinal Barrier Breakdown

evidence.reference_title: Pars Planitis: Epidemiology, Clinical Characteristics, Management and Visual Prognosis.
evidence.snippet: Cystoid macular edema is the most common cause of visual morbidity.

nodes.description: Inflammatory vascular leakage causes subclinical or clinical macular thickening that can progress to cystoid macular edema.
nodes.id: Macular Thickening from Blood-Retinal Barrier Breakdown

evidence.reference_title: A cross-sectional study of submacular thickening in intermediate uveitis and determination of treatment threshold.
evidence.snippet: The cut-off value of CFT for initiating systemic treatment was determined at 215.5 μm in a post hoc analysis (sensitivity 62.5 %, specificity 96.4 %).

evidence.reference_title: Spectral domain optical coherence tomography findings of patients with pars planitis and risk factors affecting visual acuity.
evidence.snippet: According to multivariate linear regression analysis, the presence of DME, and loss of normal foveal contour appearance at baseline were the independent factors associated with BCVA at the final examination.

nodes.description: Inflammation of peripheral retinal vessels leading to vascular sheathing and leakage.

evidence.reference_title: Pars Planitis: Epidemiology, Clinical Characteristics, Management and Visual Prognosis.
evidence.snippet: Peripheral retinal vasculitis, optic disc edema and anterior segment inflammation are other well-known findings.

evidence.reference_title: Analysis of clinical features and visual outcomes of pars planitis.
evidence.snippet: ...peripheral retinal vascular sheathing (48.1%) were the most common presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Pars Planitis: Epidemiology, Clinical Characteristics, Management and Visual Prognosis.
evidence.snippet: Although pars planitis is known to be a benign form of uveitis in most cases, it may become a potentially blinding disease due to complications including cataract, cystoid macular edema, vitreous opacities and optic disc edema.

nodes.description: Dense inflammatory cell infiltration and haze within the vitreous cavity.

evidence.reference_title: Pars Planitis: Epidemiology, Clinical Characteristics, Management and Visual Prognosis.
evidence.snippet: Diffuse vitreous cells, haze, snowballs and snowbanks are typical findings of pars planitis.

evidence.reference_title: Analysis of clinical features and visual outcomes of pars planitis.
evidence.snippet: Vitreous inflammation (100%), vitreous haze (92.6%), snowballs (74.1%), snowbanks (66.7%)...were the most common presentations.
